key_themes: Solid 2026 EPS/revenue guidance with expected Q1 beat
AI initiatives, Hypercard acquisition, and new headquarters driving growth narrative
YTD stock recovery amid premium customer resilience, offset by regulatory noise and insider selling
summary: American Express enters its April 23, 2026 Q1 earnings with consensus expectations for ~10%+ EPS and revenue growth, solid 2026 guidance ($17.30–$17.90 EPS), and positive strategic moves including an AI startup acquisition (Hypercard) and new World Trade Center headquarters.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AXP/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/american-express-axp-earnings-expected-140007447.html)[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/american-express-axp-expected-to-announce-earnings-on-thursday-2026-04-16/) The stock has recovered to ~$332 (YTD positive as of mid-April) after earlier weakness, with analysts maintaining largely overweight views and an average price target near $356.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AXP/)[[4]](https://www.marketwatch.com/investing/stock/axp/analystestimates) Biggest risk is regulatory/political pressure on credit card rates or fees (e.g., Trump comments), plus ongoing insider selling and macro sensitivity for premium spending.[[5]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/AXP/insider-trades/)[[6]](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2010715577254347076)

recent_posts.highlight: AXP falls -4% after Trump calls for 10% cap on credit card interest rates
Warren Buffett praises Amex's special position; Berkshire owns ~22% and isn't selling on rate-cap noise
AXP having its worst day since April 2025 amid AI displacement trade
Multiple posts highlight AXP as a key earnings release in a heavy week, with anticipation building
